# About this edition {.unnumbered}

Karl Kautsky, *Karl Marx’ Oekonomische Lehren. Gemeinverständlich dargestellt
und erläutert* (1886) — a complete, unabridged English translation.

## The Text

Written to make the economic theory of Marx's *Capital* accessible to a broad
working-class readership, the book became the most widely read introduction to
Marxist economics of its era. The German text is the Marxists Internet Archive
transcription of the 14th edition (Verlag von J. H. W. Dietz Nachf., Stuttgart
1912; first published 1886). Kautsky died in 1938; the German text is in the
public domain.

## Translation Principles

- **Complete and unabridged.** No clause of the German is merged, summarized,
  or omitted.
- **Consistent terminology.** Core theoretical terms are fixed: *Arbeitskraft*
  is always "labour-power", *Mehrwert* always "surplus value", and so on.
- **British spelling conventions** throughout.

## Method

The draft translation was produced with an AI-assisted pipeline (Claude,
Anthropic) constrained by a termbase and validated by mechanical checks for
omissions, markup fidelity, and terminological consistency, followed by human
review of every block.

## License

This translation is licensed under **CC BY-SA 4.0**. You are free to share and
adapt this work, provided you give appropriate credit and distribute your
contributions under the same license.

---

*This edition was translated and prepared by Douglas Campbell.*
